20
import React, { useState } from 'react';
// A simple counter component
export function Counter(props: { initial: number }) {
const [count, setCount] = useState(props.initial);
const MAX = 100;
const isEven = count % 2 === 0;
if (count > MAX) {
return <span>Limit reached</span>;
}
return (
<div>
<p>Count: {count}</p>
<p>Even: {isEven ? "yes" : "no"}</p>
<button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>Increment</button>
</div>
);
}

TokenColorSample
Source #e3e1e8c8 identifier

String #b1d36dff "hello world"

Operator #e60063ff = + - * /

Null #33b3ccff null undefined

Variable #e3e1e8e4 count data result
Parameter #8e8db3ff props args event
Property #e3e1e8e4 obj.key this.value
Function (decl) #95d4caff function myFn()
Function (call) #ffb547ff myFn() doStuff()
Method #ffb547ff arr.map() str.trim()
Built-in fn #ffb547ff console.log parseInt
Type/Class #ffb547ff MyClass React Map
Interface #93e3dbff IProps Readable
Enum #ffb547ff Status Direction

Type param #93e3dbff
